---
title: "External Resources"
---

A curated collection of external tools, guides, and organizations dedicated to child development and media.

---

### <i class="bi bi-people-fill"></i> Research-Based Guides

**UCSC CRECE Lab Resources** Direct access to our lab's website.  
[Visit Our Lab <i class="bi bi-box-arrow-up-right"></i>](https://crece.ucsc.edu/){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-xs .custom-btn-mini role="button"}

**Sesame Workshop** Excellent multimedia tools for helping children navigate trauma, grief, and big emotions.  
[Explore Sesame Tools <i class="bi bi-box-arrow-up-right"></i>](https://www.sesameworkshop.org/){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-xs .custom-btn-mini role="button"}

---

### <i class="bi bi-tv"></i> Media Literacy & Selection

**Common Sense Media** The gold standard for checking age-appropriateness and developmental themes in films and games.  
[Check Ratings <i class="bi bi-box-arrow-up-right"></i>](https://www.commonsensemedia.org/){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-xs .custom-btn-mini role="button"}

**Psychology Today: The Integrated Child** Insights into how children process narrative and digital media.  
[Read Articles <i class="bi bi-box-arrow-up-right"></i>](https://www.psychologytoday.com/){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-xs .custom-btn-mini role="button"}
---
